basic_details.job_title: ISRO ISTRAC Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 26 Technician, Technical Assistant and More Posts

basic_details.short_information: The ISRO ISTRAC Bharti 2026 is a central government recruitment drive under the Department of Space, Government of India. Positions span technical, scientific, and support roles across ISTRAC and its associated facilities including MCF Hassan, SDSC SHAR, and stations across the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. All posts are temporary and stationed at various ISRO locations across India.

age_limit.details: Each post under the ISRO ISTRAC Recruitment 2026 has distinct educational qualification and experience requirements. The age limit for most posts is 18 to 35 years as on 13 July 2026, while Cook-A falls under a different pay level structure. Candidates must verify the specific qualification requirements for each post code carefully before applying. | The age limit is calculated as on 13 July 2026. Candidates wondering about age relaxation for reserved categories should note that standard government age relaxation norms apply for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates as per Government of India rules.

content_sections.heading: ISRO ISTRAC Notification PDF Download 2026
content_sections.text: The official ISRO ISTRAC Recruitment 2026 notification (Advt. No. ISTRAC-02:2026, dated 27.06.2026) has been published on the ISRO and ISTRAC official websites. Candidates should download the full notification PDF to read complete details on posting locations, category-wise breakup for each post code, PwBD reservation specifics, and instructions for the online application process. The ISRO ISTRAC official notification PDF is accessible at the links below.

content_sections.heading: ISRO ISTRAC Recruitment 2026 - Quick Overview
content_sections.text: The ISRO ISTRAC Bharti 2026 is a central government recruitment drive under the Department of Space, Government of India. Positions span technical, scientific, and support roles across ISTRAC and its associated facilities including MCF Hassan, SDSC SHAR, and stations across the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. All posts are temporary and stationed at various ISRO locations across India.

rows.information: ISRO Telemetry Tracking and Command Network (ISTRAC), Bengaluru

Technician-B (Electronics Mechanic), Technician-B (Carpenter), Draughtsman-B (Civil), Draughtsman-B (Mechanical), Technical Assistant (Electronics), Technical Assistant (Mechanical), Scientific Assistant (Computer Science), Scientific Assistant (Mathematics), Library Assistant 'A', Cook-A

content_sections.heading: ISRO ISTRAC Notification 2026 - Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates interested in ISRO ISTRAC Vacancy 2026 must note the application window is open for approximately three weeks only. The notification was published on 27 June 2026 with the online link active on the ISRO and ISTRAC websites. Candidates should apply well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues on the portal. The last date to apply online for ISRO ISTRAC Recruitment 2026 is 20 July 2026 . The age limit for all posts is calculated as on 13 July 2026 . No offline applications will be accepted - only the online mode through the official ISRO/ISTRAC websites is valid.

content_sections.heading: ISRO ISTRAC Vacancy 2026 - Post-Wise and Category-Wise Breakdown
content_sections.text: The ISRO ISTRAC 2026 notification covers 10 post codes with a combined total of 26 vacancies spread across unreserved, OBC, SC, ST, and EWS categories. Several posts also carry PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disability) reservations. The table below gives the complete post-wise and category-wise vacancy details as available in the official notification.

rows.category_details: ST-01-identified to be posted at NESAC, Umiam. OBC- 01 - identified to be posted at ISTRAC, Sri Vijayapuram, A&N Islands SC - 01 - identified to be posted at SDSC SHAR, Andhra Pradesh UR-01, EWS-01- identified to be posted at ISTRAC, Bengaluru UR- 01 identified to be posted at ISTRAC, Thiruvananthapuram
OBC-02, LWS-01, UR-01; One post reserved for PwBD (HH - Hand of Hearing)
UR-01 earmarked for PwBD - HH (Hard of Hearing) UR-01

content_sections.heading: ISRO ISTRAC Eligibility Criteria 2026 - Qualification and Age Limit
content_sections.text: Each post under the ISRO ISTRAC Recruitment 2026 has distinct educational qualification and experience requirements. The age limit for most posts is 18 to 35 years as on 13 July 2026, while Cook-A falls under a different pay level structure. Candidates must verify the specific qualification requirements for each post code carefully before applying. The age limit is calculated as on 13 July 2026 . Candidates wondering about age relaxation for reserved categories should note that standard government age relaxation norms apply for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates as per Government of India rules.

rows.essential_qualification_and_experience: SSC/SSLC/Matriculation + ITI/NTC/NAC in Electronics Mechanic/Technician Power Electronic Systems/Mechanic Consumer Electronics/Electronic Appliances/Mechanic Industrial Electronics trade from NCVT
SSC/SSLC/Matriculation + ITI/NTC/NAC in Carpenter trade from NCVT
SSLC/Matriculation + ITI/NTC/NAC in Draughtsman (Civil) trade from NCVT
SSLC/Matriculation + ITI/NTC/NAC in Draughtsman (Mechanical) trade from NCVT
First Class Diploma in Electronics Engineering from a recognised State Board
First Class Diploma in Mechanical Engineering from a recognised State Board
First Class B.Sc. with Computer Science as main subject from a recognised University/Institution
First Class B.Sc. with Mathematics as main subject from a recognised University/Institution
Graduate in First Class + First Class Master's degree in Library Science/Library and Information Science or Equivalent from a recognised University/Institution
1. SSLC/SSC Pass or equivalent; 2. Minimum 2 years' experience in a similar capacity as cook in a well-established hotel or canteen; 3. Experience Certificates from individuals will not be accepted

content_sections.heading: ISRO ISTRAC Salary 2026 - Pay Scale and Emoluments
content_sections.text: Selected candidates under ISRO ISTRAC Vacancy 2026 will receive consolidated gross emoluments as per the 7th Pay Commission Pay Matrix. The pay level varies by post, ranging from Level 2 for Cook-A to Level 7 for Technical and Scientific Assistants and Library Assistant. All applicable central government allowances are admissible in addition to basic pay. Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

rows.post_category: Technical Assistant, Scientific Assistant, Library Assistant (Post Codes 005, 006, 007, 008, 009)

content_sections.heading: ISRO ISTRAC Apply Online 2026 - Step-by-Step Guide
content_sections.text: The ISRO ISTRAC Apply Online 2026 process is entirely online - no offline or postal applications are accepted for this recruitment. The online application link is hosted on both the ISRO headquarters website (www.isro.gov.in) and the ISTRAC website (www.istrac.gov.in). Candidates are advised to read the notification thoroughly before starting the application to avoid errors, as corrections may not be permitted after submission. Important: Applications will be received through online mode only. The link for the online registration will be hosted on the ISRO/ISTRAC websites during the period from 27 June 2026 (10:00 AM) to 20 July 2026 (05:00 PM) . Applications submitted outside this window will not be accepted.

content_sections.heading: Posting Locations - Where Will You Be Posted Under ISRO ISTRAC Recruitment 2026?
content_sections.text: One of the unique aspects of this ISRO ISTRAC Bharti 2026 is that vacancies are distributed across multiple ISRO facilities and remote locations across India. The posting location varies by post code and even by individual vacancy within the same post code. Candidates must carefully check the posting location details for each vacancy before applying, as transfers to other stations may or may not be possible after appointment. Posting locations mentioned in the notification include: ISTRAC Bengaluru , MCF Hassan (Master Control Facility) , NSRAC , Andaman and Nicobar Islands , SDSC SHAR (Satish Dhawan Space Centre) in Andhra Pradesh , and Thiruvananthapuram . For Technical Assistant (Electronics) under Post Code 005, there are 35 vacancies distributed across all these locations. Candidates residing in or willing to relocate to these locations should apply accordingly.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid - ISRO ISTRAC Apply Online 2026
content_sections.text: Applicants for ISRO ISTRAC Recruitment 2026 should be careful about the following common errors that can lead to rejection or disqualification. Applying for the wrong post code: Each post has distinct qualifications. For example, Post Code 007 (Scientific Assistant - Computer Science) and Post Code 008 (Scientific Assistant - Mathematics) have different subject requirements. Verify the correct post code before submitting. Not verifying posting location: Some post codes have vacancies at multiple locations. Ensure you are aware of where the specific vacancy is posted, as all posts are temporary and location-specific. Incorrect experience certificate for Cook-A: The notification explicitly states that experience certificates from individuals will not be accepted for Post Code 010 (Cook-A). Only certificates from established hotels or canteens are valid. Not checking ITI trade name carefully: For Technician-B posts, the ITI/NTC/NAC must specifically be in the notified trade. A closely related trade that is not listed (e.g., a general electrical trade instead of Electronics Mechanic) may not be accepted. Missing the application deadline: The online portal closes at 5:00 PM on 20 July 2026 . Waiting until the last day risks technical failure. Apply at least 2-3 days before the deadline. Uploading blurred documents: All uploaded certificates, photos, and signatures must be clearly legible. Low-resolution scans are a leading cause of application rejection. Incorrect age calculation: Age must be calculated as on 13 July 2026 , not the notification date or application date. Double-check your eligibility before applying. Not reading the PwBD reservation details: Some post codes have specific PwBD category reservations (e.g., HH for Post Code 007, OH/CH for Post Code 003). Only the specified disability categories are eligible under those reservations.

faq.question: Q1. What is ISRO ISTRAC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: ISRO ISTRAC Recruitment 2026 refers to Advertisement No. ISTRAC-02:2026 issued by ISRO Telemetry Tracking and Command Network, Bengaluru, under the Department of Space, Government of India. It covers 26 vacancies across 10 post codes including Technician-B, Draughtsman-B, Technical Assistant, Scientific Assistant, Library Assistant, and Cook-A.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for ISRO ISTRAC Vacancy 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for ISRO ISTRAC Recruitment 2026 is 20 July 2026 at 5:00 PM. The online application window opened on 27 June 2026. No offline applications are accepted.

faq.question: Q3. How many vacancies are available in ISRO ISTRAC Bharti 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 26 vacancies are available across 10 post codes. The largest single vacancy group is Technician-B (Electronics Mechanic) with 55 posts, followed by Technical Assistant (Electronics) with 35 posts.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for ISRO ISTRAC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The age limit for all posts is 18 to 35 years as on 13 July 2026. Standard government age relaxation applies for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates as per Government of India rules.

faq.question: Q5. What is the qualification for Technician-B in ISRO ISTRAC?
faq.answer: Candidates must have SSC/SSLC/Matriculation plus an ITI/NTC/NAC certificate in the relevant trade from NCVT. For Post Code 001 (Electronics Mechanic), the trade must be in Electronics Mechanic, Technician Power Electronic Systems, Mechanic Consumer Electronics, Electronic Appliances, or Mechanic Industrial Electronics.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary for ISRO ISTRAC Technical Assistant?
faq.answer: Technical Assistant posts (Post Codes 005 and 006) are at Pay Level-7 with approximate gross emoluments of Rs. 54,800/- per month plus admissible allowances as per 7th Pay Commission norms.

faq.question: Q7. Where will selected candidates be posted under ISRO ISTRAC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Posting locations vary by post code and include ISTRAC Bengaluru, MCF Hassan, NSRAC, Andaman and Nicobar Islands, SDSC SHAR (Andhra Pradesh), and Thiruvananthapuram. Candidates should verify the specific posting location for each vacancy before applying.

faq.question: Q8. Is there an application fee for ISRO ISTRAC Apply Online 2026?
faq.answer: The official notification (ISTRAC-02:2026) does not mention any application fee. Candidates should verify on the official application portal at www.isro.gov.in or www.istrac.gov.in before submitting the application.

faq.question: Q9. What is the selection process for ISRO ISTRAC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The notification does not specify the detailed selection process steps, but ISRO typically conducts a written test followed by a skill test or interview depending on the post. Candidates are advised to monitor the official ISRO and ISTRAC websites for updates on the selection schedule.

faq.question: Q10. What experience is required for the Cook-A post in ISRO ISTRAC 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates applying for Cook-A (Post Code 010) must have SSLC/SSC pass or equivalent and a minimum of 2 years' experience as a cook in a well-established hotel or canteen. Experience certificates from individuals will not be accepted — only institutional certificates are valid.
